mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales signals strong consumer spending which drives corporate earnings and lifts equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with RSAFS levels (Spearman rank correlation < 0.1 over any 2-year window).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds a level that signals overheating, the Federal Reserve is expected to tighten policy, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations over the short term.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when CPIAUCSL exceeds 260.

mechanism_narrative: A very tight labor market, indicated by high payroll employment combined with a low unemployment rate, leads to wage pressure and margin compression, negatively impacting equities over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when trigger condition holds, or the average return is not negative with 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index indicates healthy manufacturing activity and economic capacity utilization, which supports corporate profits and equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not positively correlated with INDPRO level changes (Pearson correlation coefficient < 0.1 over any 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment signals a strong labor market that boosts consumer confidence and spending, raising corporate revenues and stock prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not inversely correlated with UNRATE (Spearman rank correlation > -0.1) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above a robust threshold indicate strong residential investment and overall economic vitality, driving equity markets higher over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) on days when HOUST exceeds 1400.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales are strong and the federal funds rate is low, the economy benefits from robust demand and cheap credit, boosting equities over the next month.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong demand
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not higher than the unconditional average by at least 1% (one-sided test, p < 0.10) when trigger condition holds.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate trajectory signals a tightening cycle that increases borrowing costs and depresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 2.5 and FEDFUNDS has increased from previous value
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 2.5 and sequentially increasing.
